title: Senior Associate, Workplace Operations
description:
At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence. 

We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. 

Join EY and help to build a better working world. 

The Senior Associate, Workplace Operations will play a crucial role in overseeing and managing various workplace operations, ensuring compliance with industry standards, and enhancing the overall efficiency of our services. This position requires strong leadership skills, a keen eye for detail, and the ability to work collaboratively with internal teams and external vendors. 

Key Responsibilities: 

  1. Budget Planning: Develop and manage the workplace support services budget, ensuring effective allocation of resources and adherence to financial guidelines. 

  2. Contract Management: Oversee the management of contracts with vendors and service providers, ensuring compliance with terms and conditions and facilitating renewals or modifications as necessary. 

  3. Vendor Management: Establish and maintain relationships with third-party service providers and vendors, ensuring high-quality service delivery and compliance with contractual obligations. 

  4. Project Management: Lead and coordinate workplace-related projects, including A&A (Alterations and Additions) works, ensuring they are completed on time, within budget, and to the required quality standards. Support sustainability initiatives and manage the accreditation process for relevant BizSAFE and ISO certification documentation, ensuring compliance with industry standards and regulations. 

  5. Reporting: Prepare and present reports on workplace utilization, performance metrics, and improvement initiatives to management, ensuring transparency and informed decision-making. 

  6. Liaison with Building Management: Act as the primary point of contact with building management, ensuring effective communication and collaboration on workplace-related matters. 

  7. Process and Cost-Driven Execution: Demonstrate a process-driven and cost-effective approach to workplace support services, ensuring that all tasks are executed efficiently and effectively. 

  8. Support for In-House Events: Provide logistical support for in-house events, including setup, coordination, and post-event evaluations to ensure successful execution. 

Qualifications: 

  • Bachelor’s degree in Workplace Management, Business Administration, or a related field. 

  • Minimum of 3 years of experience in workplace support services or a related field. 

  • Strong knowledge of budget management, procurement processes, and vendor relations. 

  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively in a team environment. 

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite, with advanced Excel skills. 

  • Experience with smart locker systems and desk booking applications is a plus. 

EY | Building a better working world

EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.

Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.

EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.

postedCompany.description: EY is a global professional services organisation providing advisory, assurance, tax and transaction services. We are committed to doing our part in building a better working world for our people, our clients and our communities. And we are united by our shared values and a dedication to delivering exceptional client service.
